Container.Name: Neolink.NET
Container.Overview: Neolink.NET bridges Reolink cameras that speak the Baichuan protocol (TCP 9000) into standard RTSP streams for any NVR (Frigate, Blue Iris, VLC, Scrypted), a built-in browser UI with live view, motion/AI event review, a scrubbable timeline and recording, plus native Home Assistant entities over MQTT. No cloud, no Reolink app, zero native dependencies.[br][br][b]Setup:[/b] Just install — on first start Neolink writes a starter config.json into the Config folder and the web UI comes straight up. Open the [b]WebUI[/b] (port 8655) to create your admin account. To add cameras, edit [b]config.json[/b] in the Config path (default /mnt/user/appdata/neolink): uncomment the example camera block and set each camera's IP and the same username/password you use in the Reolink app, then restart the container.[br][br][b]Ports:[/b] 8654 RTSP (rtsp://[UNRAID-IP]:8654/<camera-name>), 8655 web UI + HTTP/WebSocket API.[br][b]Docs & full config reference:[/b] https://github.com/borexola/neolink.net
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: reolink rtsp baichuan camera nvr frigate home assistant

Config.Name: WebUI + API Port
Config.Description: Web interface and HTTP/WebSocket API. Open this in a browser to set up, watch and manage cameras.

Config.Name: RTSP Port
Config.Description: RTSP restream for NVRs and players (Frigate, Blue Iris, VLC). rtsp://[UNRAID-IP]:8654/<camera-name>

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: Holds config.json (which YOU create before first start — see the Overview) plus the web UI state (users.json, settings.json). Keep this on the cache/SSD; it stays small.

Config.Name: Recordings
Config.Description: Where event clips and 24/7 footage are written (enable per camera in the web UI, and add recording.path = /recordings to config.json). Point this at an array share with plenty of space.

Config.Name: Clips (fast tier, optional)
Config.Description: Optional SSD tier for event clips (config.json recording.clips_path = /clips). Leave empty unless you use tiered storage.

Config.Name: Archive (cold tier, optional)
Config.Description: Optional cold tier that expired footage MOVES to instead of being deleted (config.json recording.archive_path = /archive). Use a different, larger disk. Leave empty unless you use archiving.

Config.Name: Time Zone (TZ)
Config.Description: tz database name, e.g. Europe/London or America/New_York. Match your Unraid timezone so recording timestamps and the UI clock line up.
